factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal (Hormuz Reopening)
factors.summary: Major geopolitical risk-off. Pakistan PM confirmed ceasefire MOU; oil down 3-4% (Brent/WTI), equity futures jumped 0.7-1% on Globex. Strait of Hormuz reopening (formal signing Friday 19 June) removes multi-month war premium and supply-side overhang from market, unlocking liquidity back into risk assets.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse (Risk-On Catalyst)
factors.summary: Energy complex collapsed >4% on Hormuz deal news. Lower energy input costs reduce inflation pressures, supportive for growth equities and duration expansion. Energy sector drag on NDX reduces immediately.

factors.name: USD Weakness
factors.summary: DXY marked lower in thin early Asia trade post-peace announcement (per Investinglive). Weaker USD benefits mega-cap tech with heavy international revenue (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN) — historically 2-3% NDX sensitivity.

factors.name: Risk-On Momentum (Crypto Correlation)
factors.summary: Bitcoin neared $65K on peace deal narrative (per Cointelegraph). NQ-BTC correlation remains tight; sustained move into risk assets supports continued equity inflows into high-beta tech.

factors.name: Fed/Macro Calendar (Kevin Warsh First Meeting)
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh's first FOMC meeting this week presents uncertainty on forward guidance. No major inflation data (CPI, PCE) in immediate 24-48h window reduces near-term rate repricing risk, but his press conference could create volatility.

factors.name: Real Yields (Longer-Duration Sensitive)
factors.summary: No TIPS or 10Y data release in news window. With oil down and geopolitical risk premium unwinding, real yields may compress slightly, which is constructive for long-duration growth names (NVDA, TSLA, MSFT at premium multiples).

factors.name: Deal Implementation Risk
factors.summary: Iran's formal confirmation includes caveat 'finger stays on trigger' and warns of retaliation if nuclear negotiations fail. Israel conducted fresh Beirut strikes; Iran threatened heavy bombing. 30-60 day negotiation window carries execution risk — deal could unravel, triggering re-spike in oil and risk-off reversal.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO & Market Liquidity Context
factors.summary: SpaceX completed mega-IPO last week; new capital formation cycle supports risk appetite. However, this is secondary to macro risk-off unwind; no direct NDX earnings catalyst this week.

screening.narrative: US-Iran ceasefire agreement (signed Friday 19 June) is the dominant catalyst: Hormuz reopening lifts 4-month war premium, oil crashes 3-4%, and liquidity pivots back into risk assets. Mega-cap tech gains from weaker USD (FX tailwind), lower energy input costs (inflation relief), and risk-on sentiment. NAS100 futures jumped 0.7-1% on Sunday open. However, deal execution is fragile (Iran threatening retaliation, Israel conducting strikes in Lebanon) and extends over 30-60 days, meaning reversal risk remains material. Kevin Warsh's first Fed meeting this week could introduce fresh guidance uncertainty or volatility.
catalysts_ahead: Formal US-Iran deal signing ceremony (Friday 19 June, Switzerland) — execution risk
Kevin Warsh FOMC press conference (later this week) — forward guidance uncertainty
RBA & BoJ policy decisions (Monday 15-16 June) — may influence dollar direction
G7 Summit (15-17 June, France) — geopolitical commentary, potential Iran/Ukraine escalation discussion
US Industrial Production (May, due Mon 15 Jun) — secondary to macro risk-on unwind
EZ ZEW Survey (Tue 16 Jun) — European sentiment proxy
No major US inflation data (CPI/PCE) due until later week — reduces near-term rate repricing risk
screening.risk_warning: Deal unravels if Iran retaliatory strikes on Israel escalate or nuclear negotiations collapse within 30-60 day window. Oil could re-spike, unwinding risk-on trade and reversing USD weakness. Netanyahu tension with Trump ('very difficult', per Trump comment) could destabilize ceasefire. Unexpected hawkish pivot from Warsh at FOMC could trigger duration compression and growth multiple reversal. Real yield spike on surprise inflation data (CPI Tue or Wed) would invalidate bullish thesis.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (1-3 hours into catalyst reaction); NAS100 +0.7-1% on futures is still early but not extended. Enter long on pullbacks to 29,500-29,600 support (4h MA) rather than chase at resistance. Monitor Iran/Israel escalation news closely — any fresh military conflict headlines could trigger sharp reversal into 29,300s.

confirmation.entry_guidance: Initial gap higher already priced in futures (NQ +1.0% per Investinglive); wait for European open consolidation or pullback toward 29,500-29,400 support before adding longs.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal / Hormuz Reopening
factors.summary: US and Iran reached interim agreement to reopen Strait of Hormuz, halting months-long conflict. Futures gapped higher: NQ +1.0%, ES +0.7% (per Investinglive 22:03 UTC). Risk-on sentiment lifts growth/tech indices as geopolitical premium unwinds and oil supply fears ease.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: Oil slumped ~4% (Bloomberg 22:01, 22:12) on peace deal and Hormuz reopening expectations. Lower energy costs reduce input inflation for corporates, support consumer spending power, and ease stagflation concerns—all net positive for growth equities and tech multiples.

factors.name: Dollar Weakness
factors.summary: USD marked lower in thin Asia trading post-peace news (Investinglive 21:35, 22:06). Weaker dollar supports NDX constituents with heavy international revenue exposure (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA) and improves competitiveness of US tech exports.

factors.name: Real Yields / Treasury Yields
factors.summary: Oil collapse and easing geopolitical risk reduce near-term inflation expectations, likely pulling real yields lower. Lower real rates compress discount rates on long-duration growth stocks, directly benefiting high-multiple tech names in NDX.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment / Equity Futures
factors.summary: US equity futures climbed broadly (Bloomberg 22:06); Japanese stocks set to rise (Bloomberg 23:09); Asian markets cheering deal (All News 23:00). Clear risk-on rotation favors growth/tech over defensives.

factors.name: Fed Policy (Kevin Warsh FOMC This Week)
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh's first FOMC meeting Wed is high uncertainty—MarketWatch 21:00 notes 'economists honestly don't know what to expect.' Oil drop may give Fed more dovish room, but lack of Warsh track record caps confidence. Markets likely to chop into event.

factors.name: Geopolitical Execution Risk
factors.summary: Deal signing delayed to Friday (Trump per Bloomberg 22:31); Iran has not confirmed (Investinglive 21:09); Israel struck Beirut and Iran vowed retaliation (Investinglive 20:38); Trump threatened to resume strikes if nuclear accord fails (Investinglive 23:26). Implementation risk remains elevated—deal could unravel.

factors.name: Market Positioning / Phase
factors.summary: Futures already gapped +1.0% in thin Sunday Globex trading. Much of the initial 'peace premium' removal is priced. Retail chase risk into Monday cash open, but professional money may fade the gap if execution doubts emerge or Warsh uncertainty weighs.

factors.name: Crypto Correlation (BTC)
factors.summary: Crypto analyst cited by Cointelegraph (05:07) expects peace deal to send liquidity back to risk-on assets including crypto. BTC near $65K (Cointelegraph 10:06) suggests risk appetite intact, typically correlated with NDX on macro risk days.

factors.name: Upcoming Catalysts
factors.summary: RBA, BoJ, BoE, SNB decisions Mon-Wed; US Retail Sales; Japan CPI; Fed decision Wed (Newsquawk 12:43). Heavy central bank calendar could overwhelm peace deal narrative by midweek. Warsh's presser is the dominant risk event.

confirmation.narrative: US-Iran peace deal and Strait of Hormuz reopening triggered classic risk-on response: oil down 4%, USD weaker, equity futures gapping higher with NQ +1.0%. Lower oil eases inflation/stagflation fears and reduces real yields, both bullish for long-duration tech. However, the move is already underway in thin Sunday futures trading, and execution risk remains high—deal not signed until Friday, Iran hasn't confirmed, Israel-Iran tensions flaring. Kevin Warsh's first FOMC Wednesday is a major wildcard that could override the peace narrative. Bias is BUY but confidence capped at 7 due to Phase 2 entry (gap already priced) and Fed uncertainty.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC decision & Kevin Warsh first press conference (Wednesday)
Iran-US deal signing ceremony (Friday per Pakistan PM)
RBA policy decision (Tuesday)
BoJ policy decision (Tuesday)
US Retail Sales (date TBC this week)
Israel-Iran retaliation risk (immediate—Iran vowed 'heavy bombing' per Investinglive 20:38)
confirmation.risk_warning: Deal could collapse if Iran does not sign Friday or if Israel-Iran hostilities escalate (Iran threatened retaliation tonight). Kevin Warsh FOMC surprise (hawkish tilt or convoluted messaging) could reverse risk-on sentiment. If oil reverses higher or USD strengthens sharply, the bullish thesis unravels. Futures gap may fade into cash open if professional money views peace as fragile.

reason: FRESH high-conf read (7/10) — informational only, needs confirmation

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal (Risk-On Relief)
factors.summary: Announcement of US-Iran interim agreement to reopen Strait of Hormuz eliminates months of geopolitical tail-risk premium. SoftBank surged 12%+, Asian tech rallied (Taiwan +2.36%), and Kospi futures +5%. Crude WTI fell to two-month low (~$79.40), reducing inflation hedge demand and supporting growth equities.

factors.name: Dollar Weakness (DXY Softening)
factors.summary: USD Index dropped to ~99.50 as safe-haven demand faded post-deal. Weaker dollar typically benefits Nasdaq-heavy companies with international revenue and reduces real-rate pressure on growth multiples.

factors.name: Treasury Yield Compression
factors.summary: Treasuries rallied across the curve; traders trimmed Fed hike bets following Iran deal announcement. Long-duration tech extremely sensitive to falling nominal and real yields—compression supports equity multiples.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: WTI crashed to two-month low; Brent and energy complex in freefall. Lower energy costs reduce CPI/PCE inflation risks and lower odds of more aggressive Fed tightening, supporting growth equities and QQQ.

factors.name: SpaceX Nasdaq Debut (Tech Narrative Tailwind)
factors.summary: SpaceX surged past $2 trillion valuation on Friday, touching $2T+ on Nasdaq debut. Billionaire Gina Rinehart announced 'significant' SpaceX investment. Strong momentum in mega-cap/growth narrative, particularly Musk-related plays.

factors.name: Move Already in Progress (4h: -0.22%, 24h: +0.46%)
factors.summary: NAS100 is up only 46 bps in 24h despite massive overnight catalyst (Iran deal). This suggests either consolidation before larger move, or market digesting multiple cross-currents. Morning weakness despite risk-on is a warning sign—not yet full risk-on participation.

factors.name: Trump Trade War Escalation (Tariff Threat)
factors.summary: Trump threatened France with 100% wine tariffs over tech 'sales tax' ahead of G7 summit; critical minerals pricing plan faces skeptical G7 and divided industry. Tariff headlines create policy uncertainty headwind for mega-cap exporters.

factors.name: Futures Roll Date Today (Liquidity Risk)
factors.summary: CME official roll date for US equity index futures today (Monday prior to 3rd Friday). June contracts near expiry; liquidity migrates to September. Deteriorating spreads and potential chop into roll period could dampen technical follow-through.

factors.name: Israel Not Bound by Ceasefire (Geopolitical Wildcard)
factors.summary: Israeli PM Netanyahu told Trump that Israel is not bound by Lebanon ceasefire clause in MOU; Israeli forces will continue deployment in Lebanon. Undermines deal credibility and reintroduces tail-risk of conflict escalation.

factors.name: Fed Hike Path Still Intact (BOJ +1% Tuesday Omen)
factors.summary: Former BOJ economist noted BOJ hiking to 1% on Tuesday (31-year high) regardless of Iran deal; deal may reduce pressure but doesn't alter hiking cadence. US Fed still data-dependent; no major rate cuts signaled imminently.

screening.narrative: Overnight US-Iran peace deal announcement triggered a classic risk-on relief rally: equity futures jumped, oil collapsed, dollar weakened, Treasuries rallied, and Asian tech surged (SoftBank +12%, Taiwan +2.4%). However, NAS100 is up only 46 bps in 24h despite the positive news flow, suggesting either consolidation or mixed sentiment as traders digest cross-currents: Trump tariff threats on France/critical minerals, Israel's warning that it won't honor the ceasefire clause, and ongoing geopolitical tail-risk. SpaceX's successful $2T+ debut adds a bullish tech narrative tailwind, but the futures roll happening today and modest price action despite massive overnight catalyst create a cautious tone. The deal itself is not signed until Friday and involves execution risk (Iran must clear mines on Hormuz over 30 days), keeping some premium uncertainty in place.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran peace deal signing (Friday, June 20) — if delayed or terms change, risk-off reversal
G7 summit (Évian-les-Bains, France, this week) — Trump trade/tariff announcements
BOJ rate hike decision (Tuesday, June 17 EST) — +1% to 31-year high, JPY strength catalyst
US economic data (CPI, unemployment, retail sales) — if hot, Fed hike bets re-price higher
Oil reopening timeline and Iranian barrels re-entering market — watch weekly petroleum inventory
Mega-cap earnings (any guidance on capex/AI demand given weakening geopolitical premium)
Nasdaq 100 earnings calendar (especially cloud/AI plays) — hyperscaler commentary on growth
screening.risk_warning: Move is already in progress (PHASE_2) and NAS100 is only +46 bps despite massive overnight relief catalyst — this suggests either (1) skepticism on deal durability (Israel walkback is a red flag), (2) profit-taking into resistance, or (3) lack of follow-through buying. If oil stabilizes above $82 (geopolitical premium recovery), Treasury yields reverse higher, or Trump tariff threats escalate into concrete action, risk-on could quickly reverse. Futures roll today also creates liquidity gaps. Confidence is capped at 6 due to: deal not yet signed, Israel's refusal to honor ceasefire clause, modest price action despite catalyst, and upcoming BOJ hike (volatility pivot). Any intraday weak reversal below 29,400 would signal extended move reversal into pullback zone.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE_2 (fresh overnight but modest follow-through). Wait for pullback to 29,400–29,500 support or a retest of resistance at 29,900 before committing. If index breaks above 30,000 on strong volume, then enter with tight stop at 29,800. Avoid chasing into open; liquidity deteriorates on futures roll today.

factors.name: US-Iran Deal / Geopolitical De-escalation
factors.summary: US and Iran reached interim MoU to end war and reopen Strait of Hormuz, with formal signing June 19. This removes major tail risk that was weighing on equities and reduces inflation expectations from energy shock.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse & Rate-Hike Repricing
factors.summary: WTI crude dropped below $80 (3-month low) on Hormuz reopening expectations. Fed funds futures repriced to only 16 bps of tightening by year-end vs 24 bps pre-deal, directly supportive of long-duration growth stocks like Nasdaq names.

factors.name: Treasury Yield Decline
factors.summary: 10-year UST fell >4 bps to 4.441% on deal announcement and lower oil/inflation expectations. Lower real yields are structurally bullish for high-multiple tech and growth names dominating NAS100.

factors.name: Dollar Weakness
factors.summary: USD extended losses across major pairs (EURUSD, USD/JPY, USD/CAD) on risk-on sentiment and lower-for-longer rate expectations. Weaker dollar is typically bullish for multinational tech revenue and equity risk appetite.

factors.name: Risk Appetite / Equities Rally
factors.summary: S&P 500 futures up 1.3% at open; crypto and commodities rally (BTC >$65K, gold +2.86%, silver +3.94%). Broad risk-on mood supports equities and NDX-heavy growth rotation.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech / Hyperscaler Sentiment
factors.summary: Nvidia bond offering ($20B raise), SpaceX IPO +6% in premarket (now $2T+ valued), Salesforce AI agent acquisition ($3.6B Fin deal), AMD supply deal rallying Austrian supplier. Capex growth narrative intact and supported by lower rates.

factors.name: Recent Empire Fed Data (June)
factors.summary: US Empire Fed index fell sharply to +5.7 vs +14.0 expected (from +19.6 prior). New orders +3.5 vs +22.7 prior; shipments +8.6 vs +18.9 prior. Supply-chain tightness at lowest since June 2022 suggests potential slowdown, tempering growth enthusiasm.

factors.name: Near-Term Catalyst: FOMC (week of June 18)
factors.summary: Fed decision looms under new Chair Kevin Warsh. Market is now pricing 16 bps tightening by year-end (dovish repricing) post-Iran deal, but Warsh's hawkish stance or inflation persistence could alter guidance and surprise equity bulls.

factors.name: Tech Regulation / AI Curbs
factors.summary: US government cracked down on major AI model releases (implied: Anthropic or similar). Semiconductor and software stocks could face headwinds, though impact is sector-specific. Long-duration NDX still benefits from lower rates.

factors.name: Price Action: 4h/24h Context
factors.summary: NAS100 +0.46% in 24h but -0.22% in 4h, suggesting move is consolidating after initial Iran deal rip. Not extended yet; early-to-mid Phase 2.

key_levels.support: 29400–29500 (recent trading zone; Iran deal breakout floor)
key_levels.resistance: 29800–30000 (pre-deal range high; next structural level if break holds)

screening.narrative: The US-Iran deal and Hormuz reopening consensus triggered a classic risk-on unwind: oil crashed, yields fell >4 bps, USD weakened, and rate-hike expectations collapsed to 16 bps by year-end. This is structurally bullish for long-duration growth (Nasdaq's bread and butter) because lower real yields support elevated multiples on mega-cap tech. However, the concurrent sharp miss in Empire Fed (new orders -80% mom, shipments -50% mom, supply chains at 4-year lows) hints at underlying demand softness. The move is in PHASE_2—not yet extended—but the consensus is forming around lower rates and calmer geopolitics. FOMC this week under Warsh will be the critical litmus: if he holds hawkish and inflation commentary surprises, the rally could reverse quickly.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC Decision & Presser (Week of June 18, likely Wed 19th) – Warsh's forward guidance on rate path is critical; market now priced for ~16 bps tightening vs 24 bps pre-deal
BoJ Meeting (mid-week) – rate hike speculation; USD/JPY still pinned at 160+ despite Iran deal; Yen strength could weigh on risk sentiment
US-Iran Formal MOU Signing (June 19 in Switzerland) – confirmation or any friction could shift sentiment
Tech Earnings Season Ramp (Netflix, Tesla, others likely week of 23-30 June) – forward guidance on capex and AI demand critical to sustain rally
CPI/Core CPI (likely week of June 23) – inflation surprise would reprice Fed expectations sharply
screening.risk_warning: If FOMC signals hawkish hold or acceleration despite lower oil, or if CPI surprises hot, the 16 bps repricing unwinds and real yields snap higher—immediately bearish for NDX multiples. Empire Fed weakness could also accelerate into growth recession fears, killing the rally. Any Iran deal friction or Israel escalation could snap risk sentiment and reverse the USD weakness, hurting equities. Conversely, if move extends >1% without a retest, late entry risk rises materially.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is early-to-mid Phase 2 (underway ~6h). Wait for retest of 29400–29500 support zone or a 4h consolidation bar to add longs; if break above 29800, consider breakout entry but size down. Avoid chasing into the cash open; FOMC proximity (48h) warrants caution on new long positions—use tight stops.

confirmation.entry_guidance: Move already underway in pre-market (+1.3% futures per Bloomberg); wait for retest of 29,400-29,500 support zone on any pullback before adding long exposure.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Framework
factors.summary: US and Iran reached interim MOU to reopen Strait of Hormuz and end conflict, with formal signing June 19 in Switzerland. S&P 500 futures up 1.3% at 7:45am per Bloomberg; major risk-off driver removed.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: WTI crude dropped below $80 for first time since March, down to 3-month lows per CNBC. Lower energy costs = lower inflation expectations = reduced Fed hawkishness, bullish for long-duration tech multiples.

factors.name: Treasury Yields Falling
factors.summary: 10-year Treasury yield fell 4+ bps to 4.441% per CNBC as Iran deal reduced inflation/rate-hike fears. Lower nominal yields strongly bullish for high-multiple Nasdaq growth stocks.

factors.name: Fed Rate Hike Expectations Cooling
factors.summary: Market now pricing 16 bps of Fed tightening by year-end vs 24 bps before deal announcement per investing.com. Dovish repricing supports tech valuations; Warsh decision Wed looms large.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Momentum
factors.summary: SpaceX rallied 6% in premarket after record debut, now valued >$2 trillion (6th largest US company). Ron Baron bought $1B, lifting stake to $25B. Positive sentiment spillover to broader tech/growth narrative.

factors.name: M&A Activity Accelerating
factors.summary: Fox buying Roku for $22B, Salesforce acquiring Fin for $3.6B, Nuvei buying Payoneer for $2.75B, AmEx buying TheFork for $700M. Heavy M&A suggests capital deployment confidence and supports tech valuations.

factors.name: Empire Fed Manufacturing Miss
factors.summary: June Empire Fed came in at +5.7 vs +14.0 expected (prior +19.6); new orders +3.5 vs +22.7 prior. Significant slowdown in manufacturing activity, though still expanding. Negative for growth outlook but may reinforce dovish Fed pivot.

factors.name: NVDA Bond Offering
factors.summary: Nvidia seeking to raise at least $20B in first bond sale since 2021 per Bloomberg. Large capital raise could signal confidence in capex/growth plans, but also dilutes equity premium; net neutral for broader NDX.

factors.name: Broad Risk-On Sentiment
factors.summary: Gold up 2.5%, silver up 3.94%, crypto rallying (BTC >$65K per Decrypt), equities futures surging. Classic risk-on rotation after major geopolitical de-escalation favors high-beta Nasdaq.

factors.name: Dollar Weakness
factors.summary: USD lower across the board per investing.com and FXStreet as safe-haven bid unwinds. Weaker dollar bullish for NDX mega-caps with heavy international revenue exposure (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA).

factors.name: Upcoming Fed Decision
factors.summary: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's first decision looms Wednesday. Markets await policy stance; Citrini research suggests 'hot inflation/jobs mask somber reality' = potential dovish surprise, but high uncertainty caps conviction.

factors.name: China AI Rebound
factors.summary: Zhipu surged 33% as Wall Street raised bets on China AI after Anthropic curbs per CNBC. Global AI narrative broadening beyond US, positive for semiconductor/AI supply chain stocks in NDX.

confirmation.narrative: The US-Iran peace framework is the dominant catalyst, triggering a sharp risk-on rotation that strongly favors Nasdaq's long-duration tech exposure. Oil plunging to 3-month lows + falling Treasury yields + cooling Fed hike expectations = textbook bullish setup for high-multiple growth. However, S&P futures already up 1.3% in premarket (Phase 2), so much of the immediate move has occurred. Empire Fed's sharp miss adds a cautionary note on economic momentum, though it may paradoxically support dovish Fed bets. Wednesday's Warsh Fed decision is the critical next catalyst.
catalysts_ahead: Fed decision under new Chair Kevin Warsh - June 17-18 (Wednesday)
US-Iran formal agreement signing in Switzerland - June 19
Bank of Japan policy decision (rate hike expected)
Reserve Bank of Australia decision
Swedish Riksbank decision (likely hold at 1.75%)
G7 summit in France this week
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidates if: (1) US-Iran deal collapses or lacks credible verification mechanisms, reversing oil/yield moves; (2) Warsh Fed surprises hawkish Wed, signaling hikes despite lower oil; (3) Empire Fed weakness proves leading indicator of broader tech earnings deterioration; (4) futures rally fades into cash open, suggesting distribution into strength. Move already 1.3% extended in premarket - chasing here risks buying the top of initial relief rally.
